description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible joints or separations, suggesting it is a single, monolithic piece. The coloration is primarily a dark gray to black, with lighter gray tones visible on the grooved surfaces, likely due to wear or a different finish. The block is thick and robust, indicating it is designed to serve as a substantial barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped, ribbed appearance. The block is dark gray to black with lighter gray highlights on the grooves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the horizontal grooves running across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running perpendicular to the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the full width and the parallel grooves across the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the depth and regularity of the grooves.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with slight color variation on the grooves.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
